footer.builtBy: Built and maintained by Nik Reljin. Not a medical device — not a diagnosis.
footer.kofi: Was ScanContext useful? Help keep it going. ScanContext is completely free and built by one independent developer. If it saved you time or worry, please consider a one-time $10 donation — it directly funds maintenance, bug fixes, and new features. Any amount helps.
index.ai.advanced.body: For unattended / scripted setups, the same values can be written to the .env file. After editing it you must Stop, then Start ScanContext so it reloads.
index.ai.advanced.bothNote: If both are set, the in-app Settings value wins.
index.ai.advanced.summary: Advanced — set the key in .env instead
index.ai.cost.body: Cost per study is capped by design (about a dozen sampled images). Roughly 2–3¢ per scan on Flash, 10–20¢ on Pro. Even 100 studies a month is about $2–3 on Flash. Rates are set by Google.
index.ai.cost.title: What does it cost?
index.ai.lead: ScanContext works fully without AI. When you turn it on, it can summarize reports, translate non-English reports, and suggest regions of an image worth a closer look — always labelled "candidate findings, not a diagnosis". AI is off by default.
index.ai.model.body:
gemini-2.5-flash — cheap, fast, good for everyday use (the default).
gemini-2.5-pro — the most thorough analysis.
index.ai.model.title: Which Gemini model?
index.ai.privacyNote: Privacy & the Gemini free tier. ScanContext strips patient identifiers before sending anything to a cloud provider — but on Google Gemini's free tier, Google may still use your anonymized images and reports to improve its models. Only the paid tier stops that. For real medical data, use the paid tier — or use Ollama to keep everything on your machine.
index.ai.setup.step1: Get a key at aistudio.google.com/apikey — sign in, click Create API key, copy it (starts with AIza…). Keep it private.
index.ai.setup.step2: Turn on billing (paid tier) in the key's Google Cloud project — see the warning above. You can set a budget cap.
index.ai.setup.step3:
Add the key in ScanContext — no files to edit. Open ScanContext, click Settings in the top bar, then set:
  • AI provider: Gemini
  • Paste your key into Gemini API key (stored locally; the page never shows it back in full — only the last 4 characters)
  • Turn on Cloud AI enabled
  • Leave Anonymize before AI on (recommended)
  • Click Save settings
index.ai.setup.step4: Changes apply immediately — no restart needed. Then open a study and use Analyze study; you confirm cloud use per study.
index.ai.setup.step5: Your API keys are safe. Settings are protected automatically — only someone with access to your installation can change them. Keys are stored locally and never leave your machine.
index.ai.table.r1.name: Google Gemini
index.ai.table.r1.privacy: Cloud. Anonymized data sent; paid tier required for genuine privacy.
index.ai.table.r1.what: Recommended. Free tier to try; paid tier for real use.
index.ai.table.r2.name: Anthropic Claude
index.ai.table.r2.privacy: Cloud. Anonymized data sent to Anthropic.
index.ai.table.r3.name: OpenAI
index.ai.table.r3.privacy: Cloud. Anonymized data sent to OpenAI.
index.ai.table.r4.name: Ollama
index.ai.table.r4.privacy: Nothing leaves your computer. Best for privacy.
index.ai.table.r4.what: Fully local AI — no key, no internet.
index.faq.q1.body: Install Docker Desktop, open it, and wait until it reports "running". Then start ScanContext again. On macOS, make sure Docker Desktop has been launched at least once.
index.faq.q1.summary: It says "Docker is not installed" or "not running"
index.faq.q10.body: Open an issue and describe what happened: github.com/nikolareljin/scancontext/issues.
index.faq.q2.body1: If Docker Desktop reports "failed to start because virtualisation support wasn't detected", it is a Windows / PC setup issue. Two independent fixes — most people need only one.
index.faq.q2.body2: Fix A — turn on virtualization in your PC's BIOS/UEFI. Restart and press the setup key shown on the boot screen (often Del, F2, F10, or Esc). Find the virtualization setting — Intel: Intel Virtualization Technology / VT-x; AMD: SVM Mode / AMD-V — set it Enabled, save & exit, and reopen Docker Desktop.
index.faq.q2.body3: Fix B — repair the Windows features and WSL. Open Turn Windows features on or off, untick Hyper-V and Virtual Machine Platform, click OK and restart. Then open Windows PowerShell and run wsl --update (if WSL is not installed, run wsl --install as administrator) and restart. Open Docker Desktop and click Try Again.
index.faq.q2.body4: Docker's own guide: docker.com troubleshooting ↗.
index.faq.q2.summary: Windows: Docker Desktop won't start (virtualisation not detected)
index.faq.q3.body: The very first run downloads the app images — this can take a few minutes depending on your connection. Every start after that is fast.
index.faq.q3.summary: The first start is taking a long time
index.faq.q4.body: The installers aren't code-signed yet, so you may see a warning the first time. macOS: right-click the app → Open → Open. Windows: on the SmartScreen prompt, click More info → Run anyway. You only do this once.
index.faq.q4.summary: My system warns the app is unsafe / unknown
index.faq.q5.body: ScanContext uses ports 5173 and 8000. If 5173 is taken, change FRONTEND_PORT in the .env file and start again. 8000 is built into the app and must stay free — close whatever is using it rather than changing BACKEND_PORT.
index.faq.q5.summary: A port is already in use
index.faq.q6.body: No — not by default. Everything stays on your computer. Data is only ever sent out if you turn on a cloud AI provider in Settings, and even then you confirm it per study. Use Ollama to keep AI fully local too.
index.faq.q6.summary: Is my medical data uploaded anywhere?
index.faq.q7.body: No. ScanContext is not a medical device and does not diagnose anything. It helps you organize and understand your files and prepare questions. Every result — especially anything AI-suggested — must be confirmed by a qualified clinician.
index.faq.q7.summary: Can ScanContext tell me what's wrong with me?
index.faq.q8.body: In a data/ folder on your computer. With the bundle it's inside the unzipped folder; with the app it's in the per-user app data folder. Your studies are kept between runs — keep that folder and any exports private.
index.faq.q9.body: In the app, click Stop (window or tray / menu-bar icon). With the bundle, double-click Stop ScanContext.bat on Windows, or run sh ./stop.sh on macOS / Linux.
index.hero.disclaimer: Not a medical device. Not a diagnosis. ScanContext helps you organize, view, and understand medical imaging and reports. It does not diagnose any condition. Every result — especially anything AI-suggested — must be confirmed by a qualified clinician.
index.hero.h1: Understand your medical scans on your own computer.
index.hero.lead: ScanContext is a free app that opens your medical images and reports, organizes them, translates and summarizes them in plain language, and helps you prepare clear questions for your doctor — all without uploading anything.
index.hero.pill: Free · Local-first · Windows, macOS & Linux
index.hero.shotAlt: The ScanContext dashboard — import status, upload, AI provider, and recent studies.
index.hero.shotCaption: ScanContext in action — your studies, viewer, and reports together in one window.
index.how.h2: From a pile of files to a clear conversation
index.how.lead: Five simple steps. You stay in control at every one.
index.how.note: Under the hood: ScanContext runs as a small set of services inside Docker on your computer — a viewer, a report engine, and a local DICOM store. You never touch any of that; the app starts and stops it for you.
index.how.step1.body: Drag a file or a whole folder straight onto the app — DICOM studies, image files, ZIPs, PDFs, or text reports. ScanContext sorts them into studies for you, and you can delete any study when you're done.
index.how.step1.title: Import your files
index.how.step2.body: Open images and DICOM scans in a built-in viewer. Draw, mark, and save annotations to remember what you want to ask about.
index.how.step2.title: View & annotate
index.how.step3.body: Pull the text out of reports and, when needed, translate non-English reports into English — right on your machine.
index.how.step3.title: Extract & translate
index.how.step4.body: If you turn it on, AI summarizes reports and points to regions of an image worth a closer look — always clearly marked as candidates, never a diagnosis.
index.how.step4.title: AI assist (optional)
index.how.step5.body: Generate a tidy, doctor-friendly summary of the study and your questions to bring to your appointment.
index.how.step5.title: Export for your doctor
index.install.dlNote: The download buttons above always point to the newest release. Older versions are on the all-releases page.
index.install.h2: Get ScanContext running in three steps
index.install.lead: No GitHub account and no source code needed, and we'll walk you through every step. On Windows and macOS you don't touch a terminal at all. Please follow the steps in order — just pick your system in Step 2.
index.install.linux.optA.body: Pick the package for your distribution — or the portable AppImage.
index.install.linux.optA.step1: AppImage: make it executable (chmod +x) then double-click it. .deb / .rpm: install it like any package.
index.install.linux.optA.step2: Open ScanContext. It checks Docker, downloads the app on first run, then click Open ScanContext.
index.install.linux.optA.title: The ScanContext app
index.install.linux.optB.afterText: First start downloads the app (a few minutes); then your browser opens ScanContext.
index.install.linux.optB.body: Prefer the terminal? This always fetches the most recent release — no version numbers to track. Paste it into a terminal:
index.install.linux.optB.orText: Or download the bundle by hand:
index.install.linux.optB.swapNote: To download the AppImage instead, swap \.zip" for \.AppImage" in the command above.
index.install.linux.optB.tag: Option B — terminal (always gets the latest)
index.install.linux.optB.title: One-line install with curl
index.install.linux.stopNote: To stop it: click Stop in the app, or run sh ./stop.sh in the bundle folder.
index.install.mac.optA.body: For Apple Silicon Macs (M1/M2/M3 and newer). On an Intel Mac, please use Option B below.
index.install.mac.optA.step1: Open the .dmg and drag ScanContext into Applications.
index.install.mac.optA.step2: First launch: right-click the app → Open → Open (the app isn't code-signed yet — only needed once).
index.install.mac.optA.step3: It checks Docker, downloads the app on first run, then click Open ScanContext.
index.install.mac.optA.title: The ScanContext app
index.install.mac.optB.body: Works on both Apple Silicon and Intel Macs.
index.install.mac.optB.step3: First start downloads the app (a few minutes); then your browser opens ScanContext.
index.install.mac.optB.title: The download bundle (.zip)
index.install.mac.stopNote: To stop it: click Stop in the app, or run sh ./stop.sh in the bundle folder.
index.install.olderNote: Looking for a previous version, or a different file? Every release — current and older — with all of its downloads is listed here: all ScanContext releases ↗
index.install.step1.body: ScanContext runs inside Docker, so install Docker Desktop first. It is free and you only ever do this once.
index.install.step1.note: Then open Docker Desktop once and wait until it says "running". Leave it running while you use ScanContext. That's the whole of Step 1.
index.install.step1.title: Install Docker Desktop (free)
index.install.step2.body: There are two ways to run it — the app (easiest) or a download bundle. Choose whichever you prefer; both work the same once open. The buttons below download the file directly — there's no other page to visit.
index.install.step2.title: Download & start ScanContext
index.install.step3.body: When it's ready, ScanContext opens in your web browser. A short guided tour runs the first time — reopen help any time with the "?" button in the top-right corner. Import a study and explore. Your studies and data are always kept between runs.
index.install.step3.title: Use ScanContext
index.install.win.optA.body: A small desktop app that starts and stops ScanContext for you.
index.install.win.optA.step1: Double-click the downloaded installer and follow it through.
index.install.win.optA.step2: If a blue SmartScreen warning appears, click More info → Run anyway (only the first time — the app isn't code-signed yet).
index.install.win.optA.step3: Open ScanContext from the Start menu. It checks Docker, downloads the app on first run, then click Open ScanContext.
index.install.win.optA.title: The ScanContext app
index.install.win.optB.body: For developers and power users who prefer the command line. No SmartScreen warning — installs through Microsoft’s trusted channel.
index.install.win.optB.step1: Open PowerShell or Windows Terminal and run the command above.
index.install.win.optB.step2: Open ScanContext from the Start menu when the install finishes.
index.install.win.optB.title: Install via winget
index.install.win.optC.body: No installer — just unzip and double-click a start file.
index.install.win.optC.step2: Open the unzipped folder and double-click Start ScanContext.bat.
index.install.win.optC.step3: First start downloads the app (a few minutes); then your browser opens ScanContext.
index.install.win.optC.title: The download bundle (.zip)
index.install.win.stopNote: To stop it: click Stop in the app, or double-click Stop ScanContext.bat in the bundle folder.
index.os.detected: We detected {os} — the install steps below are ready for it.
index.os.generic: Install steps for Windows, macOS, and Linux are below.
index.privacy.privacy.body: Medical files contain personal health information. ScanContext keeps them on your machine; cloud AI is opt-in and strips identifiers first. That anonymization only gives real privacy on a paid provider plan — on a free tier, use Ollama for genuine medical data.
index.privacy.privacy.title: Privacy
index.privacy.safety.body: ScanContext is not a diagnostic tool and not a substitute for professional medical advice. Anything it shows — especially AI-suggested findings — is a prompt for a conversation with your doctor, never a conclusion.
index.privacy.safety.title: Safety
index.req.good.item1: Internet is needed only for the one-time first download (and for cloud AI, if you enable it)
index.req.good.item2: The app uses ports 5173 and 8000 on your machine
index.req.good.item3: No account, no sign-up, no telemetry
index.req.good.title: Good to know
index.req.need.item1: Windows 10/11, macOS 12 or newer, or a modern Linux desktop
index.req.need.item3: About 4 GB free disk space, plus room for your studies
index.req.need.item4: A modern browser — Chrome, Edge, Firefox, or Safari
index.req.need.title: You need
index.support.h2: ScanContext is free — and built by one person
index.support.lead: If it saved you time or worry, a one-time $10 donation keeps it maintained and improving. Any amount helps.
index.tour.gallery1.alt: ScanContext dashboard: drag-and-drop upload area, studies, and AI provider status.
index.tour.gallery1.caption: Drag a file or a whole folder to import; your studies, import status, and AI provider at a glance. Delete a study anytime.
index.tour.gallery2.alt: The ScanContext dashboard in dark theme.
index.tour.gallery2.caption: Light, Dark, or System theme — switch from the header or Settings.
index.tour.gallery3.alt: The ScanContext image viewer showing a CT slice.
index.tour.gallery3.caption: Scroll slices, zoom, and adjust window/level in the viewer.
index.tour.gallery4.alt: A CT slice with an annotation box overlaid.
index.tour.gallery4.caption: Confirmed marks in solid blue; AI suggestions in dotted amber.
index.tour.gallery5.alt: The report panel showing an imported radiology report.
index.tour.gallery5.caption: Reports shown as imported, with optional translation and summary.
index.tour.gallery6.alt: The AI review tab with the Analyze study action.
index.tour.gallery6.caption: Optional AI review — candidate findings, never a diagnosis.
index.tour.gallery7.alt: The export tab with the Anonymize option enabled.
index.tour.gallery7.caption: Export a doctor-friendly ZIP — anonymized if you choose.
index.tour.lead: Everything below is the real app, captured from its built-in demo study — a fully fictional CT chest scan with invented patient details. No real medical data is shown.
index.tour.videoFallback: Your browser can't play this video — download the walkthrough.
index.what.card1.body: Runs on your own computer. Your scans and reports never leave it unless you deliberately turn on a cloud AI provider.
index.what.card1.title: 100% local-first
index.what.card2.body: No medical or technical background needed. Plain-language summaries, guided tour, and a help button on every screen.
index.what.card2.title: For patients & families
index.what.card3.body: ScanContext works fully without AI. When you choose to enable it, results are always labelled "candidate findings, not a diagnosis".
index.what.card3.title: AI is optional
index.what.h2: A calm, private place for confusing medical files
index.what.lead: A CD of scan images, a folder of DICOM files, a PDF report in a language you don't read — medical records arrive in formats made for hospitals, not for you. ScanContext opens all of them in one window, keeps them on your machine, and explains them in plain language.
index.why.card1.body: Nothing is uploaded by default. Files live in a folder on your computer that you can see and back up.
index.why.card1.title: Your data stays home
index.why.card2.body: Turns dense radiology wording into something you can actually read, and translates foreign-language reports.
index.why.card2.title: Plain language
index.why.card3.body: DICOM, JPEGs, PDFs, ZIP archives, text reports — all opened in one place, no extra software.
index.why.card3.title: One viewer for everything
index.why.card4.body: Built by one independent developer. Free, with an optional small donation if it helps you.
index.why.card4.title: Free to use
index.why.card5.body: Windows, macOS, and Linux — the same app, the same simple steps on every system.
index.why.card5.title: Works everywhere
index.why.card6.body: AI is off until you switch it on, and you confirm cloud use for each individual study.
index.why.card6.title: You decide on AI
index.why.card7.body: Walk into your appointment with an organized summary and a clear list of questions.
index.why.card7.title: Ready for the doctor
index.why.card8.body: A desktop app with a tray / menu-bar icon starts and stops ScanContext for you — no scripts to run.
index.why.card8.title: One-click app
index.why.card9.body: The first start downloads the app once. Every start after that is quick.
index.why.card9.title: Fast after first run
meta.description: ScanContext is a free, local-first medical image viewer, report organizer, and optional AI-assisted annotation tool for Windows, macOS, and Linux. Your data stays on your machine. Not a medical device.
meta.ogDescription: Free, local-first medical image viewer and report organizer for Windows, macOS, and Linux. Your data stays on your machine. Not a medical device.
meta.ogTitle: ScanContext — understand your medical scans on your own computer
meta.title: ScanContext — understand your medical scans on your own computer
privacy.ai.body1: ScanContext supports optional AI-assisted annotation and analysis. These features are disabled by default and require explicit configuration by the user.
privacy.ai.body2: When you configure an AI provider, ScanContext may send image data and report text to that provider in order to generate AI-assisted findings. You are in full control of this: nothing is sent until you enable and configure a provider.
privacy.ai.body3: Supported provider types and their data handling:
privacy.ai.item1: Local AI (Ollama and compatible tools): All processing happens on your own machine. No data leaves your computer.
privacy.ai.item2: External AI providers (e.g. Google Gemini, OpenAI-compatible APIs): Data is sent to the provider's servers. This is subject to the provider's own privacy policy. Review your chosen provider's terms before using AI features with sensitive medical data. Using a paid provider tier is generally required for meaningful data handling commitments.
privacy.ai.warnNote:
Medical data and external AI providers.

ScanContext may strip identifying metadata before sending data to cloud AI providers, but anonymization is not a guarantee of privacy. If you are working with real patient data or personally identifiable health information, use a local AI provider such as Ollama, or consult your data protection obligations before enabling cloud AI features.
privacy.collect.body: ScanContext does not collect or transmit any personal data on its own. The only network activity the application initiates is pulling Docker images from GitHub Container Registry (GHCR) on first run — this is handled by Docker itself, not by application code, and is limited to downloading the software needed to run.
privacy.collect.item1: Docker image pulls (GHCR): Docker contacts ghcr.io to download the ScanContext container image on first run and when updates are available. This is subject to GitHub's privacy policy.
privacy.collect.note: All other data — DICOM files, medical images, reports, annotations, settings — is stored exclusively on your local machine.
privacy.contact.body1: If you have questions about this privacy policy or about how ScanContext handles data, please open an issue at:
privacy.contact.body2: This policy may be updated from time to time. The effective date at the top of this page reflects when the current version took effect. Continued use of ScanContext after a policy update constitutes acceptance of the revised policy.
privacy.contact.disclaimerNote:
Not a medical device.

ScanContext is a personal productivity tool for organizing and reviewing medical imaging files. It is not a diagnostic device, not a medical device, and not a substitute for professional medical advice. Always consult a qualified healthcare provider for medical decisions.
privacy.hero.meta:
Last updated: May 2026  ·  Effective date: May 2026
Publisher: Nikola Reljin  ·  App identifier: com.nikolareljin.scancontext
privacy.meta.description: ScanContext privacy policy: what data is collected, what is not, and how your medical files stay on your machine.
privacy.meta.title: Privacy Policy — ScanContext
privacy.notCollect.item1: No telemetry: No usage statistics, crash reports, or diagnostic data are sent anywhere.
privacy.notCollect.item2: No analytics: No Google Analytics, Mixpanel, Sentry, or any other third-party analytics tool is integrated into the app.
privacy.notCollect.item3: No accounts: ScanContext has no sign-up, no login, and no user profiles. There is no server that knows you exist.
privacy.notCollect.item4: No medical data uploads: Your DICOM files, medical images, and reports are never uploaded to any server by default.
privacy.notCollect.item5: No behavioral tracking: No information about how you use the app is recorded or transmitted.
privacy.overview.body: ScanContext is a local-first desktop application. Your medical images, DICOM files, reports, and annotations never leave your machine unless you explicitly configure an external AI provider. There are no accounts, no telemetry, and no analytics — in the app or on this website.
privacy.overview.note:
One exception: AI features.

If you configure an external AI provider (such as Google Gemini), ScanContext will send image and report data to that provider when you use AI-assisted features. This is always opt-in — the app does not contact any AI service by default.
privacy.rights.body1: Because ScanContext stores all data locally on your machine, you have complete control over it. Uninstalling the application and deleting the application data directory removes all data associated with ScanContext from your system. There is no server-side data to request, export, or delete.
privacy.rights.body2: If you used an external AI provider while ScanContext was configured to do so, any data retained by that provider is subject to their own data retention and deletion policies.
privacy.thirdParty.body: The following external services may be involved depending on how you interact with ScanContext. Each is governed by its own privacy policy.
privacy.thirdParty.footerNote: The ScanContext landing website (this page) uses GitHub Pages for hosting. It contains no cookies, no analytics scripts, and no tracking of any kind.
privacy.thirdParty.item1: GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io) — GitHub, Inc.: Used to distribute Docker images. Contacted by Docker on first run and for updates. GitHub Privacy Statement
privacy.thirdParty.item2: Ko-fi: The donation link on the ScanContext website points to a Ko-fi page. If you choose to donate, Ko-fi processes that transaction. Ko-fi Privacy Policy
privacy.thirdParty.item3: GitHub Issues: The primary support channel for ScanContext is GitHub Issues at github.com/nikolareljin/scancontext/issues. Use of GitHub is subject to GitHub's privacy policy.
